Those who have turned away from God willingly, and together, encouraging one another to sin, should, willingly and together, return to him. This will bring glory to God and benefit them. It will be very helpful for strength during times of suffering, and to encourage our repentance, to maintain good thoughts about God, and his purposes and plans concerning us. Freedom from trouble should feel like coming back to life. God will restore them; the certainty of this should motivate them to return to him. But this seems to have a further connection to the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Let us admire the wisdom and goodness of God that when the prophet foretold the deliverance of the church from her troubles, he pointed to our salvation by Christ; and now these words are fulfilled in the resurrection of Christ, it confirms our faith that this is He who should come, and we are to look for no other. Here is a precious blessing promised: this is eternal life, to know God. The return of God’s favor is secured to us as firmly as the return of morning after a dark night. He will come to us as the latter and former rain to the earth, which refreshes it and makes it fruitful. The grace of God in Christ is both the latter and the former rain; and by it the good work of our producing fruit begins and continues. And just as the Redeemer was raised from the grave, so He will revive the hearts and hopes of all who trust in him. Even a small glimpse of hope in his word is a sure promise of increasing light and comfort, which will be accompanied by purifying, comforting grace that makes fruitful.

Sometimes Israel and Judah seemed inclined to repent under their suffering, but their goodness disappeared like an empty morning cloud, and the early dew, and they were as bad as ever. Therefore, the Lord sent serious messages through the prophets. The word of God will bring about either the death of sin or the death of the sinner. God desired mercy rather than sacrifice, and that knowledge of him which produces holy fear and love. This exposes the foolishness of those who trust in outward practices to make up for their lack of love for God and people. Just as Adam broke God’s covenant in paradise, so Israel had broken his national covenant, despite all the favors they received. Judah also was ready for divine judgment. May the Lord put his fear into our hearts, establish his kingdom within us, and never leave us to ourselves, nor allow us to be overcome by temptation.
